name: security-gate
description: Verify security considerations were addressed before shipping. Issues result in WARNINGS that strongly recommend fixing.
capabilities: Provides security review checklists for code
Asks targeted questions about input validation
Checks for proper authentication and authorization
author: DanielPodolsky
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing security guidelines and checklists. The static scanner detected patterns in educational examples showing what NOT to do (anti-patterns), not actual malicious code. No executable code, network calls, or file system access beyond reading its own documentation file.
valueStatement: Security vulnerabilities often slip through code reviews unnoticed. This gate provides systematic security checks to catch common issues like SQL injection, XSS, and authorization flaws before they reach production.
limitations: Does not replace professional security audits

useCases.title: Pre-deployment Security Checks
useCases.description: Run security gate before merging pull requests to catch vulnerabilities early in development cycle.

useCases.title: Security Code Review Guide
useCases.description: Use structured questions to ensure all security aspects are reviewed during code reviews.

useCases.title: Learn Security Best Practices
useCases.description: Understand common security vulnerabilities and how to prevent them through practical examples.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Security Review
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm building a login form. Run the security gate to check for common vulnerabilities I should address.

promptTemplates.title: API Security Check
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a REST API with user endpoints. Use the security gate to review authentication and data access patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Full Application Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a complete security review of my Node.js application using all security gate questions and checklists.

promptTemplates.title: Database Security
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my SQL queries and database access patterns for injection vulnerabilities and proper authorization.

bestPractices: Run security gate on every pull request before merging
Address all CRITICAL warnings before deployment
Use the Socratic questions to guide team discussions
Keep security checklists updated with new threats
antiPatterns: Assuming security is only for production systems
Relying solely on automated tools without human review
Skipping security reviews for simple changes

faq.question: What security issues does this gate check for?
faq.answer: It checks for SQL injection, XSS, authorization flaws, insecure data exposure, hardcoded secrets, and other common vulnerabilities.

faq.question: Does this replace a professional security audit?
faq.answer: No, it is a pre-deployment check for common issues. Critical applications still need professional security testing.

faq.question: How long does a security review take?
faq.answer: Typically 5-15 minutes depending on code complexity. Critical findings may require longer discussion.

faq.question: Can I customize the security questions?
faq.answer: The gate provides standard questions, but you can add project-specific security requirements as needed.

faq.question: What if the gate finds too many issues?
faq.answer: Focus on CRITICAL and HIGH risk issues first. Fix MEDIUM and LOW issues in subsequent iterations.

faq.question: Is this suitable for all programming languages?
faq.answer: The principles apply universally, though examples focus on web technologies. Adapt questions for your stack.
